conjecture_title: Open Problem: polynomial-time 4-colorability on fixed surfaces
paper_title: Three-coloring triangle-free graphs on surfaces I. Extending a coloring to a disk with one triangle
summary: The question of whether 4-colorability of graphs on a fixed surface (other than the sphere) admits a polynomial-time algorithm remains open as of 2026. By contrast, 5-colorability on any fixed surface can be decided in linear time (via finiteness of 6-critical graphs), and 3-colorability of triangle-free graphs on any fixed surface can be decided in linear time (Dvořák, Král, Thomas, Part VII of this series). The structural paper arXiv:1801.10457 on irreducible 4-critical triangle-free toroidal graphs provides structural insight but does not resolve the algorithmic question. No follow-up paper resolving this problem was found in the published literature.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper characterizes 4-critical triangle-free irreducible toroidal graphs via computer-assisted structural enumeration; it addresses structural properties (bounding number and sizes of faces) but does not resolve the polynomial-time 4-colorability question.

search_queries: polynomial-time algorithm 4-colorability graphs fixed surface Dvorak Kral Thomas
4-colorability graphs surface NP-hard fixed surface complexity
4-critical graphs fixed surface polynomial algorithm Dvorak 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
arxiv 4-colorability fixed surface polynomial NP-complete Dvorak 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: The analogous 3-colorability (triangle-free) and 5-colorability questions on fixed surfaces are both resolved in polynomial (linear) time. The 4-colorability question is explicitly stated open by the authors with the remark that the techniques currently available do not give much hope for a positive resolution in the near future. No follow-up paper resolving this problem was found after exhaustive search.
